2056.name: A Forested Slope
2056.description: Sloping sharply, the dense forest thins out as the elevation declines to the south, and becomes thicker upon the high ground to the north. The thin band of forest rakes a large cliff from top to bottom as it travels from north to south, and the trees in which you stand are about half way up. Thus, the cliff rises to the east, and drops off to the west.

npcs.name: Grunk
npcs.description: Part goblin and part, well, who knows what, but something disgusting.

2057.name: A Bend in the Trail
2057.description: The dark, shadowed trail bends to follow a wooden wall to the northeast, continuing its course through the forest to the north and east. A cliff to the west is diagonally raked by a sloping band of forest, descending southwards, and a few trees grow atop the cliff just to the west.

npcs.name: Lorak
npcs.description: Wearing the furs of a barbarian, he appears quite primitive.  He is obviously half-elven, for his slender elven features are complimented by a

2058.name: Shadowed Trail
2058.description: The haunting silence of the forest preys on your mind, blacking out your very thoughts. Wary to keep your wits about you, you peer around. The dark trail continues its trek through the forest to the north and south, and the forest to the west lies open to exploration. The wooden wall of the eastern forest blocks entry, but appears to cease its southward course further down the trail.

2059.name: Shadowed Trail
2059.description: The wooden wall running through the forest just east of here is badly blackened, as if a fire of great ferocity was hurled against it. The wall seems to have withstood the attack, and still serves to prevent passage into the forest beyond. The trail of soft earth upon which you stand leads north and south through the forest, and with some effort, you could break through the bushes of the forest to the west.

2060.name: Flat-Stone Bridge
2060.description: Long granite slabs have been laid across a river here to form a crude but effective bridge. To the west, the river forms a flat, flowing pool, as if before a waterfall. The current comes from the east from beneath a wooden wall, preventing access to the forest beyond by means of boat. The dark trail leading to the bridge delves back into the forest to the north and south, forming your only direction of travel other than leaping off the bridge into the chilling water below.

2061.name: Shadowed Trail
2061.description: Padding quietly through the thin forest trail, you cannot suppress the feeling that eyes follow your every movement. You glance nervously over your shoulder... but nothing stirs. Perhaps it`s just your imagination playing tricks on you, yet you cannot rid yourself of the sliver of doubt that nags at you from your subconsciousness.

npcs.name: Talinesian patrol captain
npcs.description: He stands firm with a steady brow, ready to destroy any Erinesian he

npcs.name: Talinesian patroler
npcs.description: She wears a mask of calm to hide her obvious pain.  From the looks of her tattered clothing, she has been in a recent battle, and perhaps lost

2062.name: Shadowed Trail
2062.description: The trail continues to wind through the silent forest; the dense forest represses all noise save for that of your soft footfalls. Treading beneath the formidable firs, you notice that some of them bear faintly discernible markings upon their bark, a remnance of travelers of old. A towering wooden wall blocks travel through the forest to the east, but you could likely squeeze between the trees to the west.

2063.name: Forest Outskirts
2063.description: A thin sprinkling of trees meets the base of a steep rocky mountain slope here. An arduous journey could be made up the slope to reach the snow-covered peaks seen far above in the clouds. A wooden wall to the east runs through the sparse trees and meets the sheer face of the mountain, blocking entrance to the lush forest beyond. However, the forest spreads to the south and can be entered there.

npcs.name: Lorak
npcs.description: Wearing the furs of a barbarian, he appears quite primitive.  He is obviously half-elven, for his slender elven features are complimented by a

2672.name: Shadowed Trail
2672.description: A gap in the northern wooden wall lies to the north, allowing travel to the lush forest beyond. Numerous footprints cover the ground beneath the firs, as if entire armies swept northward between the trees. The thin, shadowed trail continues it trek through the gloomy trees to the east and west.

n.to_room_name: At an Outpost in the Forest

2673.name: Shadowed Trail
2673.description: The spikes of the wooden wall to the north guard the trees beyond, turning aside all thoughts of clambering over to the forest beyond. The gloomy, dense forest to the south appears less inviting, but enterable all the same. The trail winds eastward and westward.

2674.name: Shadowed Trail
2674.description: Deep ruts cut the trail here, indicating that recent wagons have left the trail from the west and turned southward, entering the bleak forest. Most likely a formidable task, as the trail is barely wide enough for a cart, let alone the dense trees to the south. Lush, healthy trees grow beyond a wooden wall to the north.

2675.name: Shadowed Trail
2675.description: A break in the wooden wall to the north allows travel into the lush Erinesian forest beyond. The dense, gloomy forest of Talinesia lies somewhat less invitingly to the south. The trail that forms the border between the two continues its course to the east and west.

npcs.name: Talinesian patrol captain
npcs.description: He stands firm with a steady brow, ready to destroy any Erinesian he

npcs.name: Talinesian patroler
npcs.description: She wears a mask of calm to hide her obvious pain.  From the looks of her tattered clothing, she has been in a recent battle, and perhaps lost

2676.name: Shadowed Trail
2676.description: The trail winds peacefully through the silent forest, devoid of noise save for that of nearby birds. Atop the cliff to the southeast, a vast forest of firs stretches indefinitely eastward and southward, and joins the forest in which you stand further along the trail to the east.
